name: neatnet
documentation:
# `neatnet`: Street Geometry Processing Toolkit

[![Continuous Integration](https://github.com/uscuni/neatnet/actions/workflows/testing.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/uscuni/neatnet/actions/workflows/testing.yml) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/uscuni/neatnet/graph/badge.svg?token=GFISMU0WPS)](https://codecov.io/gh/uscuni/neatnet)

## Introduction

## Documentation

## Examples

```py
import neatnet

simplified = neatnet.simplify_network(gdf)
```

## Contribution

See our guidelines in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/uscuni/neatnet/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Recommended Citation

---------------------------------------

This package developed & and maintained by:
* [Martin Fleischmann](https://github.com/martinfleis)
* [Anastassia Vybornova](https://github.com/anastassiavybornova)
* [James D. Gaboardi](https://github.com/jGaboardi)

Copyright (c) 2024-, neatnet Developers

functions.name: neatnet.continuity.continuity
documentation:
Assign COINS-based information to roads.

# Parameters

roads

: Road network.

angle_threshold

: See the `angle_threshold` keyword argument in `momepy.COINS()`.

# Returns

roads

: The input `roads` with additional columns describing COINS information.

coins

: **This is not used in production.**

# Notes

The returned `coins` object is not used in production, but is
very helpful in testing & debugging. See gh:neatnet#49.

functions.name: neatnet.continuity.get_stroke_info
documentation:
Generate information about strokes within `artifacts` and the
resulting lists can be assigned as columns to `artifacts`. Classifies
the strokes within the CES typology.

> - 'continuing' strokes - continues before and after artifact.
> - 'ending' strokes - continues only at one end.
> - 'single' strokes - does not continue.

# Parameters

artifacts

: Polygons representing the artifacts.

roads

: LineStrings representing the road network.

# Returns

strokes

: All strokes counts.

[c]

: Counts for 'continuing' strokes - continues before and after artifact.

[e]

: Counts for 'ending' strokes - continues only at one end.

[s]

: Counts for 'single' strokes - does not continue.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.split
documentation:
Split lines on new nodes.

# Parameters

split_points

: Points to split the `cleaned_roads`.

cleaned_roads

: Line geometries to be split with `split_points`.

crs

: Anything accepted by `pyproj.CRS`.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon for point snapping.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoSeries | geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: Resultant split line geometries.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes._snap_n_split
documentation: Snap point to edge and return lines to split.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes._status
documentation: Determine the status of edge line(s).

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.get_components
documentation:
Associate edges with connected component labels and return.

Parameters
----------
edgelines : list | np.ndarray | gpd.GeoSeries
    Collection of line objects.
ignore : None | gpd.GeoSeries = None
    Nodes to ignore when labeling components.

Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    Edge connected component labels.

Notes
-----
See [https://github.com/uscuni/neatnet/issues/56] for detailed explanation of
output.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.weld_edges
documentation:
Combine lines sharing an endpoint (if only 2 lines share that point).
Lightweight version of `remove_false_nodes()`.

# Parameters

edgelines

: Collection of line objects.

ignore

: Nodes to ignore when welding components.

# Returns

list | np.ndarray | gpd.GeoSeries

: Resultant welded `edgelines` if more than 1 passed in, otherwise
  the original `edgelines` object.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.induce_nodes
documentation:
Adding potentially missing nodes on intersections of individual LineString
endpoints with the remaining network. The idea behind is that if a line ends
on an intersection with another, there should be a node on both of them.

# Parameters

roads

: Input LineString geometries.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon for point snapping passed into `nodes.split()`.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: Updated `roads` with (potentially) added nodes.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes._identify_degree_mismatch
documentation: Helper to identify difference of observed vs. expected node degree.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes._makes_loop_contact
documentation:
Helper to identify:
1. loop nodes intersecting non-loops
2. loop nodes intersecting other loops

functions.name: neatnet.nodes._loops_and_non_loops
documentation: Bifurcate edge gdf into loops and non-loops.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.remove_false_nodes
documentation:
Reimplementation of `momepy.remove_false_nodes()` that preserves attributes.

# Parameters

gdf

: Input edgelines process. If any edges are `MultiLineString` they
  will be exploded into constituent `LineString` components.

aggfunc

: Aggregate function for processing non-spatial component.

kwargs

: Keyword arguments for `aggfunc`.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoSeries | geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: The original input `gdf` if only 1 edgeline, otherwise the processed
  edgeline without interstitial nodes.

# Notes

Any 3D geometries are (potentially) downcast in loops.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes._rotate_loop_coords
documentation: Rotate loop node coordinates if needed to ensure topology.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.fix_topology
documentation:
Fix road network topology. This ensures correct topology of the network by:

> 1. Adding potentially missing nodes...
>    : on intersections of individual LineString endpoints
>      with the remaining network. The idea behind is that
>      if a line ends on an intersection with another, there
>      should be a node on both of them.
> 2. Removing nodes of degree 2...
>    : that have no meaning in the network used within our framework.
> 3. Removing duplicated geometries (irrespective of orientation).

# Parameters

roads

: Input LineString geometries.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon for point snapping passed into `nodes.split()`.

kwargs

: Key word arguments passed into `remove_false_nodes()`.

# Returns

gpd.GeoDataFrame

: The input roads that now have fixed topology and are ready
  to proceed through the simplification algorithm.

functions.name: neatnet.nodes.consolidate_nodes
documentation:
Return geometry with consolidated nodes.

Replace clusters of nodes with a single node (weighted centroid
of a cluster) and snap linestring geometry to it. Cluster is
defined using hierarchical clustering with average linkage
on coordinates cut at a cophenetic distance equal to `tolerance`.

The use of hierachical clustering avoids the chaining effect of a sequence
of intersections within `tolerance` from each other that would happen with
DBSCAN and similar solutions.

# Parameters

gdf

: GeoDataFrame with LineStrings (usually representing street network).

tolerance

: The maximum distance between two nodes for one to be considered
  as in the neighborhood of the other. Nodes within tolerance are
  considered a part of a single cluster and will be consolidated.

preserve_ends

: If `True`, nodes of a degree 1 will be excluded from the consolidation.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoSeries

: Updated input `gdf` of LineStrings with consolidated nodes.

modules.name: neatnet.geometry
documentation: Geometry-related functions

functions.name: neatnet.geometry._is_within
documentation:
Check if the line is within a polygon with a set relative tolerance.

# Parameters

line

: Input line to check relationship.

poly

: Input polygon to check relationship.

rtol

: The set relative tolerance.

# Returns

np.ndarray

: `True` if `line` is either entirely within `poly` or if
  `line` is within `` poly` `` based on a relaxed `rtol` relative tolerance.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry.angle_between_two_lines
documentation:
Return the angle between 2 two lines (assuming they share a vertex).
Based on `momepy.coins` but adapted to shapely lines.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry.voronoi_skeleton
documentation:
Returns average geometry.

# Parameters

lines

: LineStrings connected at endpoints. If `poly` is passed in, `lines`
  must be a `geopandas.GeoSeries`.

poly

: Polygon enclosed by `lines`.

snap_to

: Series of geometries that shall be connected to the skeleton.

max_segment_length: float | int = 1

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

buffer

: Optional custom buffer distance for dealing with Voronoi infinity issues.

secondary_snap_to

: Fall-back series of geometries that shall be connected to the skeleton.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework, we clip to fit inside the polygon.
  To ensure we get a space to make proper topological connections from the
  linework to the actual points on the edge of the polygon, we clip using a
  polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit` or the radius of
  maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the resulting skeleton.
  If `None`, no consolidation happens.

# Returns

edgelines

: Array of averaged geometries.

splitters

: Split points.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry._remove_sliver
documentation: Remove sliver(s) if present.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry._as_parts
documentation: Return constituent LineStrings if MultiLineString present.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry._consolidate
documentation: Return `edgelines` from consolidated nodes, if criteria met.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry.snap_to_targets
documentation:
Snap edgelines to vertices.

# Parameters

edgelines

: Voronoi skeleton edges.

poly

: Polygon enclosed by `lines`.

snap_to

: Series of geometries that shall be connected to the skeleton.

secondary_snap_to

: Fall-back series of geometries that shall be connected to the skeleton.

# Returns

to_add, to_split

: Lines to add and points where to split.

functions.name: neatnet.geometry._prep_components
documentation: Helper for preparing graph components & labels in PySAL.

documentation: Helper for preparing splitter points & added lines.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify._link_nodes_artifacts
documentation: Helper to prep nodes & artifacts when simplifying singletons & pairs.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify._classify_strokes
documentation: Classify artifacts with `{C,E,S}` typology.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify._identify_non_planar
documentation: Filter artifacts caused by non-planar intersections.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.simplify_singletons
documentation:
Simplification of singleton face artifacts – the first simplification step in
the procedure detailed in `simplify.simplify_loop()`.

This process extracts nodes from network edges before computing and labeling
face artifacts with a `{C, E, S}` typology through `momepy.COINS` via the
constituent road geometries.

Next, each artifact is iterated over and constituent line geometries are either
dropped or added in the following order of typologies:

> 1. 1 node and 1 continuity group
> 2. more than 1 node and 1 or more identical continuity groups
> 3. 2 or more nodes and 2 or more continuity groups

Non-planar geometries are ignored.

# Parameters

artifacts

: Face artifact polygons.

roads

: Preprocessed road network data.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.
  Used in multiple internal geometric operations.

compute_coins

: Flag for computing and labeling artifacts with a `{C, E, S}` typology through
  `momepy.COINS` via the constituent road geometries.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon used in multiple internal geometric operations.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework, we clip to fit inside the
  polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper topological connections
  from the linework to the actual points on the edge of the polygon, we clip
  using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit` or the radius of
  maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

simplification_factor

: The factor by which singles, pairs, and clusters are simplified. The
  `max_segment_length` is multiplied by this factor to get the
  simplification epsilon.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation when generating Voronoi skeletons.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: The road network line data following the singleton procedure.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.simplify_pairs
documentation:
Simplification of pairs of face artifacts – the second simplification step in
the procedure detailed in `simplify.simplify_loop()`.

This process extracts nodes from network edges before identifying non-planarity
and cluster information.

If paired artifacts are present we further classify them as grouped by
first vs. last instance of duplicated component label, and whether
or not to be simplified with the clustered process.

Finally, simplification is performed based on the following order of typologies:
: 1. Singletons – merged pairs & first instance (w/o COINS)
  2. Singletons – Second instance – w/ COINS
  3. Clusters

# Parameters

artifacts

: Face artifact polygons.

roads

: Preprocessed road network data.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.
  Used in multiple internal geometric operations.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework, we clip to fit inside the
  polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper topological connections
  from the linework to the actual points on the edge of the polygon, we clip
  using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit` or the radius of
  maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

simplification_factor

: The factor by which singles, pairs, and clusters are simplified. The
  `max_segment_length` is multiplied by this factor to get the
  simplification epsilon.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation when generating Voronoi skeletons.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: The road network line data following the pairs procedure.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.simplify_clusters
documentation:
Simplification of clusters of face artifacts – the third simplification step in
the procedure detailed in `simplify.simplify_loop()`.

This process extracts nodes from network edges before iterating over each
cluster artifact and performing simplification.

# Parameters

artifacts

: Face artifact polygons.

roads

: Preprocessed road network data.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.
  Used in multiple internal geometric operations.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon used in multiple internal geometric operations.

simplification_factor

: The factor by which singles, pairs, and clusters are simplified. The
  `max_segment_length` is multiplied by this factor to get the
  simplification epsilon.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation when generating Voronoi skeletons.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: The road network line data following the clusters procedure.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.get_type
documentation:
Classify artifact edges according to the `{C, E, S}`
schema when considering solutions for pairs of artifacts.

# Parameters

edges

: Artifact edges in consideration.

shared_edge

: The index location of the shared edge of the pair.

# Returns

str

: Classification for an edge in `{C, E, S}`.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.get_solution
documentation:
Determine the solution for paired planar artifacts.

Parameters
----------
group : geopandas.GeoDataFrame
    Dissolved group of connected planar artifacts.
roads : geopandas.GeoDataFrame
    Road network data.

Returns
-------
pandas.Series
    The determined solution and edge to drop.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.simplify_network
documentation:
Top-level workflow for simplifying networks. The input raw road network data is
first preprocessed (topological corrections & node consolidation) before two
iterations of artifact detection and simplification.

Each iteration of the simplification procedure which includes (1.) the removal
of false nodes; (2.) face artifact classification; and (3.) the line-based
simplification of face artifacts in the order of single artifacts, pairs of
artifacts, clusters of artifacts.

For further information on face artifact detection and extraction
see {cite}`fleischmann2023`.

# Parameters

roads

: Raw road network data.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.
  Used in multiple internal geometric operations.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework, we clip to fit inside the
  polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper topological connections
  from the linework to the actual points on the edge of the polygon, we clip
  using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit` or the radius of
  maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

simplification_factor

: The factor by which singles, pairs, and clusters are simplified. The
  `max_segment_length` is multiplied by this factor to get the
  simplification epsilon.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation when generating Voronoi skeletons.

artifact_threshold

: When `artifact_threshold` is passed, the computed value from
  `momepy.FaceArtifacts.threshold` is not used in favor of the
  given value. This is useful for small networks where artifact
  detection may fail or become unreliable.

artifact_threshold_fallback

: If artifact threshold detection fails, this value is used as a fallback.

area_threshold_blocks

: Areal theshold for block detection.

isoareal_threshold_blocks

: Isoareal theshold for block detection.
  See `esda.shape.isoareal_quotient`.

area_threshold_circles

: Areal theshold for circle detection.

isoareal_threshold_circles_enclosed

: Isoareal theshold for enclosed circle detection.
  See `esda.shape.isoareal_quotient`.

isoperimetric_threshold_circles_touching

: Isoperimetric theshold for enclosed circle touching.
  See `esda.shape.isoperimetric_quotient`.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon used in multiple internal geometric operations.

exclusion_mask

: Polygons used to determine face artifacts to exclude from returned output.

predicate

: The spatial predicate used to exclude face artifacts from returned output.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: The final, simplified road network line data.

functions.name: neatnet.simplify.simplify_loop
documentation:
Perform an iteration of the simplification procedure which includes:
: 1. Removal of false nodes
  2. Artifact classification
  3. Simplifying artifacts:
     : - Single artifacts
       - Pairs of artifacts
       - Clusters of artifacts

# Parameters

roads

: Raw road network data.

artifacts

: Face artifact polygons.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.
  Used in multiple internal geometric operations.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework, we clip to fit inside the
  polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper topological connections
  from the linework to the actual points on the edge of the polygon, we clip
  using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit` or the radius of
  maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

simplification_factor

: The factor by which singles, pairs, and clusters are simplified. The
  `max_segment_length` is multiplied by this factor to get the
  simplification epsilon.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation when generating Voronoi skeletons.

eps

: Tolerance epsilon used in multiple internal geometric operations.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: The road network line data following 1 iteration of simplification.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.get_artifacts
documentation:
Extract face artifacts and return the FAI threshold.
See {cite}`fleischmann2023` for more details.

# Parameters

roads

: Input roads that have been preprocessed.

threshold

: First option threshold used to determine face artifacts. See the
  `artifact_threshold` keyword argument in `simplify.simplify_network()`.

threshold_fallback

: Second option threshold used to determine face artifacts. See the
  `artifact_threshold_fallback` keyword argument in
  `simplify.simplify_network()`.

area_threshold_blocks

: Areal theshold for block detection.

isoareal_threshold_blocks

: Isoareal theshold for block detection.
  See `esda.shape.isoareal_quotient`.

area_threshold_circles

: Areal theshold for circle detection.

isoareal_threshold_circles_enclosed

: Isoareal theshold for enclosed circle detection.
  See `esda.shape.isoareal_quotient`.

isoperimetric_threshold_circles_touching

: Isoperimetric theshold for enclosed circle touching.
  See `esda.shape.isoperimetric_quotient`.

exclusion_mask

: Polygons used to determine face artifacts to exclude from returned output.

predicate

: The spatial predicate used to exclude face artifacts from returned output.

# Returns

artifacts

: Face artifact polygons.

threshold

: Resultant artifact detection threshold from `momepy.FaceArtifacts.threshold`.
  May also be the returned value of `threshold` or `threshold_fallback`.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.ccss_special_case
documentation:
If there are primes on both ``` C``s, connect them. If there's
prime on one ``C ```, connect it to the other `C`. If there are
no primes, get midpoints on C\`\`s and connect those.

# Parameters

primes

: Nodes that are external to the artifacts, but need to be kept

conts_groups

: All `C` labeled edges dissolved by connected component label.

highest_hierarchy

: `edges` in the `C` continuity group – `edges[~es_mask]`.

relevant_targets

: The nodes forming the artifact.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

# Returns

numpy.ndarray

: New linestrings for reconnections.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.filter_connections
documentation:
The skeleton returns connections to all the nodes. We need to keep only
some, if there are multiple connections to a single C. We don't touch the other.

# Parameters

primes

: Nodes that are external to the artifacts, but need to be kept

relevant_targets

: The nodes forming the artifact.

conts_groups

: All `C` labeled edges dissolved by connected component label.

new_connections

: New linestrings for reconnections.

# Returns

tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]
: - Updated `new_connections`
  - Connections intersecting `C`
  - Connections intersecting `primes`

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.avoid_forks
documentation:
Multiple C\`\`s that are not intersecting. Avoid forks on the ends of a
Voronoi skeleton. If one goes to a relevant node, keep it. If not, remove
both and replace with a new shortest connection.

# Parameters

highest_hierarchy

: `edges` in the `C` continuity group – `edges[~es_mask]`.

new_connections

: New linestrings for reconnections.

relevant_targets

: The nodes forming the artifact.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

# Returns

np.ndarray

: `new_connections` with either 1 prong of the fork if it connects to
  a relevant node or a new short connection if not.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.reconnect
documentation:
Check for disconnected Cs and reconnect.

# Parameters

conts_groups

: All `C` labeled edges dissolved by connected component label.

new_connections

: New linestrings for reconnections.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

eps

: Small tolerance epsilon.

# Returns

np.ndarray

: `new_connections` with additional edges.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.remove_dangles
documentation:
Dropping lines can introduce dangling edges. Remove those.

# Parameters

new_connections

: New linestrings for reconnections.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

eps

: Small tolerance epsilon.

# Returns

np.ndarray

: `new_connections` without dangling edges.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.one_remaining
documentation:
Resolve situations where there is 1 highest hierarchy and 1
remaining node. This function is called within `artifacts.nx_gx()`:

> - first SUBRANCH of BRANCH 2:
>   : - relevant node targets exist
>       : - only one remaining node

# Parameters

relevant_targets

: The nodes forming the artifact.

remaining_nodes

: Nodes associated with the artifact that are not in group `C`.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

es_mask

: A mask for `edges` in the `E` and `S` continuity groups.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the
  `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: Newly resolved edges. The `split_points` parameter is also updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.multiple_remaining
documentation:
Resolve situations where there is 1 highest hierarchy and multiple
remaining nodes. This function is called within `artifacts.nx_gx()`:

> - second SUBRANCH of BRANCH 2:
>   : - relevant node targets exist
>       : - more than one remaining node
> - second SUBRANCH of BRANCH 3:
>   : - no target nodes - snapping to C
>       : - more than one remaining node

# Parameters

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

es_mask

: A mask for `edges` in the `E` and `S` continuity groups.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

highest_hierarchy

: `edges` in the `C` continuity group – `edges[~es_mask]`.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

snap_to

: Snap to these relevant node targets.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the
  `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`.

# Returns

geopandas.GeoDataFrame

: Newly resolved edges. The `split_points` parameter is also updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.one_remaining_c
documentation:
Resolve situations where there is 1 highest hierarchy and 1 remaining node.
This function is called within `artifacts.nx_gx()`:

> - first SUBRANCH of BRANCH 3:
>   : - no target nodes - snapping to C
>       : - only one remaining node

# Parameters

remaining_nodes

: Nodes associated with the artifact that are not in group `C`.

highest_hierarchy

: `edges` in the `C` continuity group – `edges[~es_mask]`.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

es_mask

: A mask for `edges` in the `E` and `S` continuity groups.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the
  `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`.

# Returns

numpy.ndarray

: Newly resolved edges. The `split_points` parameter is also updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.loop
documentation:
Replace an artifact formed by a loop with a single line formed
by a subset of the Voronoi skeleton.

# Parameters

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

es_mask

: A mask for `edges` in the `E` and `S` continuity groups.

highest_hierarchy

: `edges` in the `C` continuity group – `edges[~es_mask]`.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

eps

: Small tolerance epsilon.

# Returns

to_add

: Linestring geometries to be added.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.n1_g1_identical
documentation:
Determine lines within artifacts to drop & add when dealing with typologies
of 1 node and 1 continuity group – `{C, E, S}`.

# Parameters

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

to_drop

: List collecting geometries to be dropped.

to_add

: List collecting geometries to be added.

geom

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

# Returns

None

: `to_drop` and `to_add` are updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.nx_gx_identical
documentation:
Determine lines within artifacts to drop & add when dealing with typologies of
more than 1 node and 1 or more identical continuity groups – `{C, E, S}`.

It is used when there are at least two nodes, one or more continuity groups but all
edges have the same position in their respective continuity groups. For example,
they all are `E` (ending), or `S` (single). It does not mean that all edges
belong to a single continuity group. Here the "identical" refers to identical
continuity groups – e.g. ``` 4CCC, ``5EE ```, or `3SSS`.

Drop all of them and link the entry points to a centroid.

# Parameters

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

geom

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

to_drop

: List collecting geometries to be dropped.

to_add

: List collecting geometries to be added.

nodes

: Node geometries forming the artifact.

angle

: Threshold for determination of line intersection angle acuteness.
  If the angle between two lines is too sharp, replace with a
  direct connection between the nodes.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the
  `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`.

eps

: Small tolerance epsilon.

# Returns

None

: `to_drop` and `to_add` are updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.nx_gx
documentation:
Determine lines within artifacts to drop & add when dealing with typologies of
2 or more nodes and 2 or more continuity groups – `{C, E, S}`.

Drop all but highest hierarchy. If there are unconnected nodes after drop, connect
to nearest remaining edge or nearest intersection if there are more remaining edges.
If there are three or more of the highest hierarchy, use the roundabout solution.

If, after dropping, we end up with more than one connected component based on
remaining edges, create a connection either as a shortest line between the two or
using a skeleton if that is not inside or there are 3 or more components.

The connection point should ideally be an existing
nearest node with degree 4 or above.

# Parameters

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

artifact

: The polygonal representation of the artifact.

to_drop

: List collecting geometries to be dropped.

to_add

: List collecting geometries to be added.

split_points

: Points to be used for topological corrections.

nodes

: Node geometries forming the artifact.

clip_limit

: Following generation of the Voronoi linework in `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`,
  we clip to fit inside the polygon. To ensure we get a space to make proper
  topological connections from the linework to the actual points on the edge of
  the polygon, we clip using a polygon with a negative buffer of `clip_limit`
  or the radius of maximum inscribed circle, whichever is smaller.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the
  `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`.

eps

: Small tolerance epsilon.

# Returns

None

: `to_drop` and `to_add` are updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.nx_gx_cluster
documentation:
Determine lines within artifacts to drop & add when dealing with typologies of
*clusters* of 2 or more nodes and 2 or more continuity groups – `{C, E, S}`.

Here $n$-artifact cluster are treated as follows:
: - merge all artifact polygons;
  - drop all lines fully within the merged polygon;
  - skeletonize and keep only skeletonized edges and connecting nodes

# Parameters

edges

: Line geometries forming the artifact.

cluster_geom

: The polygonal representation of the artifact cluster.

nodes

: Node geometries forming the artifact.

to_drop

: List collecting geometries to be dropped.

to_add

: List collecting geometries to be added.

max_segment_length

: Additional vertices will be added so that all line segments
  are no longer than this value. Must be greater than 0.

min_dangle_length

: The threshold for determining if linestrings are dangling slivers to be
  removed or not.

consolidation_tolerance

: Tolerance passed to node consolidation within the
  `geometry.voronoi_skeleton()`.

eps

: Small tolerance epsilon.

# Returns

None

: `to_drop` and `to_add` are updated inplace.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.is_dangle
documentation: Determine if an edge is dangling or not.

functions.name: neatnet.artifacts.line_segments
documentation: Explode a linestring into constituent pairwise coordinates.
